task_completion.description: Agent investigates the original containers to identify security issues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing commands like `docker inspect acme-webapp`, `docker inspect acme-gateway`, or `docker inspect acme-monitor` and their JSON output revealing the misconfigurations.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the fixed replacement containers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing `docker run` commands used to start `acme-webapp-fixed`, `acme-gateway-fixed`, and `acme-monitor-fixed`, or `docker ps` output showing these three new containers are currently running.

task_completion.description: Agent stops the original vulnerable containers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing `docker stop` commands for `acme-webapp`, `acme-gateway`, and `acme-monitor`, or `docker ps` output confirming the original containers are no longer in the list of running containers.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the incident report file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with a file named `incident_report.txt` or an icon for `incident_report.txt` visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The incident report documents the specific findings, impact, and remediation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in `incident_report.txt` explicitly mentions excessive capabilities (e.g., SYS_ADMIN, NET_ADMIN) for acme-webapp, hardcoded secrets/environment variables for acme-gateway, and a dangerous host bind mount (/etc) for acme-monitor.

integrity.description: Agent actually ran docker commands in the terminal to investigate and remediate.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history shows genuine `docker inspect`, `docker run`, and `docker stop` commands rather than the agent just guessing the issues and writing a report.

integrity.description: The report only mentions the actual security issues present in the containers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The incident report focuses on the capabilities, environment variables, and bind mounts, without hallucinating unrelated vulnerabilities (like outdated image versions or missing security patches).

integrity.description: The report contains explanations of impact and remediation, not just a copy-paste of the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in `incident_report.txt` includes custom written analysis of the potential impact of the exploits and the steps taken to fix them.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The three containers have specific security issues: 1) acme-webapp has excessive Linux capabilities (SYS_ADMIN, NET_ADMIN, SYS_PTRACE). 2) acme-gateway has hardcoded secrets in environment variables (DATABASE_PASSWORD, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, INTERNAL_API_TOKEN). 3) acme-monitor has a dangerous bind mount of the host /etc directory (/etc:/host-etc:ro). The fixed containers must be named acme-webapp-fixed, acme-gateway-fixed, and acme-monitor-fixed. The report must be saved at ~/Desktop/incident_report.txt.
